name: auth.deviceLogin
description: Initiate OAuth 2.0 device authorization flow (RFC 8628) for CLI/IDE authentication. Returns device code and user code for browser-based consent, then polls until user approves or denies access.

client_id.description: OAuth client identifier for the requesting application.

poll_interval.description: Polling interval in seconds. Server may enforce minimum (default 5s per RFC 8628).

timeout.description: Maximum time in seconds to wait for user authorization before giving up.

store_tokens.description: Whether to persist tokens in system keychain (or file fallback). Recommended for persistent authentication.

status.description: Final authorization status after polling completes.

device_code.description: Device code generated by authorization server (internal use for polling).

user_code.description: Short human-readable code user enters in browser (e.g., 'ABCD-1234').

verification_uri.description: URL where user navigates to enter user_code and grant consent.

verification_uri_complete.description: Optional URL with user_code pre-filled for one-click consent flow.

expires_in.description: Device code validity period in seconds (typically 900s per RFC 8628).

interval.description: Minimum polling interval in seconds enforced by server.

access_token.description: OAuth access token issued after successful authorization.

refresh_token.description: OAuth refresh token for obtaining new access tokens without re-authentication.

scopes.description: Granted scopes (may differ from requested if user modified consent).

expires_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when access token expires.

refresh_expires_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when refresh token expires.

token_storage_path.description: Location where tokens are stored (keychain service name or file path).

error.description: RFC 8628 error code if authorization failed or polling timed out.
